Two AA biologics, totally different immunology. 
 
One blocks IL-7R and depletes effector/memory T-cells.   
The other expands Tregs and depletes nothing. 
 
For a lifelong disease you’d potentially dose for decades, that distinction matters. 
 
Long $NKTR — the data should speak for itself.

DCTH.body: $DCTH Some information was provided in the last call about the farthest advanced of the Phase 2 trials, which addresses mCRC liver cancer dominant. The aim is to activate all 26 of the planned trial sites by the end of 2026. 13 sites are currently active with a total of seven patients. There was also a mention of interim data from this trial at some point next year.

$DCTH The current target for sites approved for Hepzato in uveal melanoma is 37 in 2026, and 40 by Q1 of 2027. My impression from the last call was that that number is likely to increase. CEO Michel said that he had talked with as many as 48 institutions, and that there was tremendous interest. Right now there are 31 sites.
$DCTH the addition of Sutter Health is pretty significant. They were listed on the HepzatoKit website in January 2025 and only treated their first patient in July 2026 - 18 months.
$DCTH grants equity awards to four new hires under Nasdaq Rule 5635(c)(4). https://www.rapidticker.com/news/dcth-delcath-systems-inc-announces-inducement-fdc470
$DCTH Sutter Health now listed as 31st active REMS HEPZATO site
the shorts are running away from $DCTH
$DCTH BTIG today raised the price target from $16 to $17.

$DCTH has announced that the first patient has been dosed at the European Institute of Oncology (IEO) in Milan, Italy, in its global Phase 2 clinical trial evaluating HEPZATO in combination with standard of care treatment for liver-dominant metastatic breast cancer (mBC). 
 
https://biotuesdays.com/2026/07/09/first-patient-dosed-in-delcaths-global-trial-of-hepzato-in-mbc/

$DCTH 
 
Another interesting setup showing why price action matters. 
 
Different sectors, different stories, but the same trading principle keeps appearing: respect the levels, watch the structure, and let the chart confirm the move. 
 
$DCTH has been following the technical setup closely, with buyers stepping in at key areas and momentum continuing to develop. 
 
This is why I focus on process over predictions. Good setups can appear anywhere when you understand trend, support, and momentum. 
 
The market always creates opportunities. The goal is being ready when they show up.

$DCTH first patient dosed in Phase 2 HEPZATO trial for liver-dominant HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer. This is Delcath’s shot to show HEPZATO is a platform, not a single-indication play. Watching data and timelines closely. 
#DCTH #Biotech #Oncology #HEPZATO 
https://prismmarketview.com/delcath-systems-starts-phase-2-hepzato-trial-in-liver-dominant-metastatic-breast-cancer/

- SUNRISE-FA 2 pivotal study for LX2006 on track to initiate in Q2 2026 with topline data expected in 2H 2027

$LRMR  
 
LXEO spikes today. 
 
FA has a very small market size, but it seems there are more and more companies offer drugs in FA treatment. 
 
$LXEO   and $DSGN and $BIIB  are the 3 companies in addition to LRMR.
$LXEO issued a press release detailing regulatory updates for its LX2006 gene therapy targeting Friedreich ataxia cardiomyopathy.

JPMorgan🏁 $LRMR and said: We are initiating coverage of Larimar Therapeutics (LRMR) with an Overweight rating and a Dec 2027 price target of $9. 
$PTCT SLDB BIIB DSGN $LXEO 
Larimar's lead asset is nomlabofusp (nomla; CTI-1601), a subcutaneously administered, recombinant fusion protein designed to deliver frataxin (FXN) to the mitochondria of patients with Friedreich's ataxia (FA), a rare neurodegenerative disorder. 
 
In the near-term, in 2Q26, we expect to see topline results from the ongoing open-label (OL) study as well as feedback from the company's type B meeting with the FDA to align on the overall BLA package readiness. 
 
The company expects a rolling BLA submission for accelerated approval (AA) in June 2026, pending FDA feedback, using skin FXN levels as a surrogate endpoint, with the final submission including the CMC module expected in 2H26; if approved, nomla would be the first FXN replacement therapy for FA, with a potential U.S. approval and launch in 1H27.

$LEXX Human Study #7 is officially completed 🤝

Now that the active patient dosing window is completely wrapped up, the transition to data compilation and unblinding is officially underway.

Here is the expected countdown schedule for the late-summer data drops:

Late August 2026: Human Study #7 (GLP-1-H26-7) data drops. This remains the headline event comparing DehydraTECH tablets and capsules head-to-head against commercial Wegovy.

 Late August / Early September 2026: Animal Study #2 (GLP-1-A26-2) data drops. This massive 18-arm study evaluates how DehydraTECH pairs with tomorrow's multi-agonist blockbusters—Eli Lilly's retatrutide and Novo Nordisk's amycretin.

 Early September 2026: Animal Study #1 (GLP-1-A26-1) data drops. This 11-arm study will show performance results for alternative, non-SNAC formulation carriers and map out drug absorption directly into brain tissue.

Everything is compressed into roughly a 3-to-4 week window 🚀 

Let’s make a deal 💰 📈 

$LLY $NVO $RANI

RPID.body:
$RPID On the face of it, I love RPID's automation idea.   However, after some thought and research following the last presentation with Amgen, I see issues with this type of disruptive technology. 
1) RPID's special plates claim to have a longer dessication profile, which for the most part has been countered with the big microbiology companies offering extra-filled agar plates, which takes away from their savings equation. 
2) The time to implementation is longer than I expected (in years), giving other automation companies runway to improve microbiology operations with less disruption. 
3) Relationships with existing vendors is a large barrier in the microbiology world.  (Specifically calling out the inertia within microbiology) 
4) What happen's if RPID cannot supply their proprietary plates?   Can labs that transition fully to RPID transition back to normal plates and not disrupt plant operations? (Risk)
